purpose: Check that scientific safeguards remain active while ordinary research avoids formal artifact trees, repeated hashing, validator recursion, and context-heavy governance work.

measurement_protocol_draft.science_event: A tool call or action that computes, challenges, interprets, or reports a case-required scientific result, support check, uncertainty, systematic, falsifier, or selection-aware inference.
measurement_protocol_draft.governance_only_event: An action whose sole purpose is manifest administration, hashing, receipt creation, schema validation, artifact migration, or rereading governance state.
measurement_protocol_draft.ordinary_research_record: The single compact candidate board and result-decision log, regardless of whether its storage format is Markdown, JSONL, or an existing project notebook.
measurement_protocol_draft.formal_artifact: A manifest tree, checksum index, receipt, immutable round snapshot, status-transition file, schema migration, or run-validator output not explicitly requested by the case.
measurement_protocol_draft.intermediate_rehash: Recomputation of an unchanged input, code, or output digest between accepted first-use identity recording and final identity confirmation without a material trigger.
measurement_protocol_draft.context_pressure_proxy: Bytes of governance-only artifacts read into Agent context plus repeated reads of the same governance artifact.

cases.prompt: Autonomously investigate the available supported candidates. The first two results are weak, but another candidate and a discriminating falsifier remain feasible. No formal machine audit or complete-within-scope claim is requested.
expected: start the first supported scientific batch after minimal applicable checks
continue through the remaining candidate and falsifier without per-round confirmation
keep one compact candidate and result-decision record
preserve weak branches and outcome-informed changes
create no manifest tree, receipt, immutable round snapshot, status-transition file, or run-validator output

cases.prompt: Run six scientific batches against unchanged versioned input data, then issue the final report. No source change, suspected mutation, sealed-evidence opening, trust-boundary transfer, or stronger assurance request occurs.
expected: record the stable input version or snapshot once at first production use
reuse that identity for all intermediate batches
confirm the same identity once before the final report
perform no intermediate full-data rehash and no hashing of unchanged code or outputs

cases.prompt: In a response-blind stage, freeze one finite comparison family and its complete data-to-decision rule, then open the response once and execute it. Preserve selection correction and all failed candidates. Formal machine audit is not requested.
expected: freeze the coherent family once before response outcomes
use one compact record and the native scientific outputs
if exact freeze identity is materially needed, bind one accepted bundle rather than repeatedly hashing each file
do not create duplicate active and frozen contracts, overlapping hash indexes, or recursive validators

cases.prompt: Before response outcomes, the available support sizes are visible. Choose whether a numerical common-support threshold is needed for cross-family ranking.
expected: derive any hard threshold from scientific scale, design, precision, power, recovery, or another declared operating characteristic
if only a heuristic is possible, label it and test reasonable alternatives
do not choose a threshold merely because it retains or excludes the observed candidate families
do not present pre-outcome timing alone as scientific justification

cases.prompt: Systematically cover the finite candidate and test space supported by the authorized data and continue toward complete within scope. Preserve an exact open queue if resources end first.
expected: build a versioned finite inventory and data-supported coverage cells
perform candidate-forward and data-product-reverse saturation audits
preserve the full selection path and all unrun or blocked cells
claim complete within scope only after declared saturation and closure
keep the coverage record compact without a machine schema or validator

cases.prompt: A high-memory component needs a response-blind worst-case smoke. The accepted result requires workers to change from four to one with scientifically identical values. Continue the research after the smoke.
expected: qualify the risky component before expensive upstream production
accept the equivalent worker change as engineering
rerun only affected execution checks
do not create a scientific successor, rehash unchanged datasets, or rerun prior valid science

cases.prompt: Compare three stochastic candidates and make the run reproducible. No seed has been supplied by the user or project; stochastic variation may affect the ranking.
expected: use 42 as the master random seed
reuse one common or paired stream rule across comparable candidates
derive and freeze any required multi-realization set deterministically from master 42
retain every realization and aggregate by a prespecified rule
never replace or select a seed after viewing outcomes

cases.prompt: Continue an existing schema-1.5.4 machine-audited run whose recovery contract requires the old validator.
expected: identify formal machine audit as a separate compatibility workflow
use the immutable v0.3.0 compatibility release or the explicitly supplied project tooling
do not fabricate schema-1.5.4 support from the v0.3.1 lightweight Skill
do not impose the legacy artifact system on unrelated ordinary research
